articles.title: Mouni Roy disables Instagram comments amid divorce news, Disha Patani dragged into online trolling
articles.description: Mouni Roy has restricted comments on her Instagram posts after social media users began speculating about trouble in her marriage and dragged her BFF and actor Disha Patani into the online chatter.

articles.title: CCPA Imposes Penalty on Coaching Institutes for misleading advertisements and unfair trade practices
articles.description: New Delhi: The Central Consumer Protection Authority (CCPA) has passed final orders against Motion Education Pvt. Ltd., imposing a penalty of ₹10 lakh, and against Career Line Coaching (CLC), Sikar, imposing a penalty of ₹5 lakh, for indulging in misleading advertisements, unfair trade practices, and violation of consumer rights under the Consumer Protection Act, 2019. [...]

articles.title: If Keir Starmer Quits As PM, Would Britain Face A General Election?
articles.description: More than 90 Labour lawmakers have now reportedly either called for Starmer to resign or asked him to set out a clear timetable for his departure.

articles.title: ‘We are against Hindutva, not Hindus’: Vijay’s TVK minister asks Udhayanidhi to clarify Sanatan Dharma remarks
articles.description: Tamil Nadu minister and TVK general secretary Aadhav Arjuna on Thursday adopted a cautious approach to Udhayanidhi’s controversial remarks.

articles.title: Paranjpe was inducted in Shiv Sena to stop him from joining MVA; NCP was kept in loop: Uday Samant
articles.description: Former NCP leader Anand Paranjpe has joined the Shiv Sena. Maharashtra minister Uday Samant revealed the move was to keep Paranjpe from joining opposition parties. The NCP, led by Sunetra Pawar, was informed about the induction. This was done to prevent any misunderstandings within the ruling Mahayuti alliance. Paranjpe previously left the Shiv Sena and later joined the NCP.

articles.title: D K Shivakumar wishes Kerala CM-designate Satheesan, says he was picked from among 'capable senior leaders'
articles.description: DK Shivakumar: Karnataka Deputy CM D K Shivakumar congratulated Kerala CM-designate V D Satheesan after the Congress-led UDF sweep. He said Satheesan was chosen from among capable senior leaders in the race for the top post.

articles.title: BIG BREAKING: MP High Court Indore Bench to deliver verdict on Bhojshala dispute today at 2:30 PM
articles.description: The Madhya Pradesh High Court’s Indore Bench is set to deliver a crucial verdict today at around 2:30 PM on the long-standing Bhojshala dispute in Dhar. The site is claimed by Hindu, Muslim, and Jain groups, with Hindu पक्ष identifying it as a Saraswati temple, Muslim Party as Kamal Maula mosque, and Jain community also presenting its claim. The case includes findings from an ASI survey and extensive court hearings, after which the judgment was reserved. Currently, Bhojshala sees a unique arrangement where Hindu prayers are held on Tuesdays and Muslim prayers on Fridays. Ahead of the verdict, heavy security measures have been deployed across Dhar, including police deployment, drone surveillance, and Section 163 to prevent any law-and-order situation. Authorities are on high alert to ensure peace regardless of the court’s decision

articles.title: "Family Knows": Thailand's Singha Beer Heir Claims Sexual Abuse By Brother
articles.description: Siranudh said his mother accuses him of being ungrateful for publicly sharing details about alleged abuse

articles.title: India can play long-term mediator role: Russian Foreign Minister Lavrov on Iran-US conflict
articles.description: Russia's Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov proposed India as a mediator for the Iran-US conflict. He highlighted India's extensive diplomatic experience and its role as BRICS chair. Lavrov suggested India could facilitate dialogue between Iran and its Arab neighbours to prevent regional instability. India's past mediation efforts in various global conflicts were also noted.

articles.title: Keir Starmer to be ousted as UK PM? How a leadership challenge could unfold
articles.description: Dozens of MPs have called on UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer to step down or at least set out a timetable for his exit. Now, a number of challengers have emerged including ex-health secretary Wes Streeting and Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham

articles.title: MoS Margherita visits Dominica, pays tribute to Mahatma Gandhi in Belize
Former Kerala CM Pinarayi Vijayan slams Centre for hiking fuel prices
articles.description: Pinarayi Vijayan criticizes the Centre for fuel price hikes, claiming it harms public welfare and exacerbates the cost-of-living crisis.

articles.title: After NEET paper leak, Karnataka CM seeks return of state-controlled medical entrance tests
articles.description: Siddaramaiah said the Karnataka government was insisting that powers to conduct such entrance examinations should be restored to states.

articles.title: Rahul Gandhi links Adani relief in US report to India FTA, targets PM Modi: ‘Not trade deal, but a bargain’
articles.description: Invoking his earlier “compromised PM” jibe, Rahul Gandhi said, “PM did not strike a trade deal, but a bargain for Adani's release.”

articles.title: Devendra Fadnavis rides a Bullet, Shinde takes EV, other Maha ministers walk after PM Modi's austerity push
articles.description: Maharashtra's leaders, including CM Fadnavis, demonstrated austerity by using motorcycles and electric vehicles.

articles.title: Chennithala returns to capital, greeted by supporters; leaves home before VD Satheesan arrives
articles.description: After offering prayers at Guruvayur Temple, Chennithala reached Thampanoor Railway Station, where Congress workers gave him a warm reception.

articles.title: 'Bindas Aram Karo': Manager Offers Period Leave To New Employee, Sparks Debate Online
articles.description: A chat between a manager and a female employee about period leave has sparked debate online, with many discussing workplace sensitivity and office culture

articles.title: Jaishankar, Iranian foreign min hold bilateral talks on sidelines of Brics
articles.description: The high-level interaction took place on the sidelines of the Brics Foreign Ministers' Meeting in New Delhi, marking a pivotal moment in the bilateral trajectory between the two nations

articles.title: Man spots dead bee in Domino’s pizza, Punjab consumer court orders Rs 10,000 aid for poor patients
articles.description: The Mohali district consumer commission had partly allowed a complaint filed by advocate Gurpyar Singh and another customer, awarding them Rs 1 lakh for mental agony, harassment and litigation expenses.

articles.title: 'Why Are You Here Again?' SC Pushes Settlement In Sunjay Kapur Estate Row, Flags Concern For 80YO Mother
articles.description: 'Why Are You Here Again?' SC Pushes Settlement In Sunjay Kapur Estate Row, Flags Concern For 80YO Mother

articles.title: SC flags ‘executive veto’ in CEC appointment
articles.description: The Supreme Court questioned the "executive veto" in appointing election commissioners, asserting it undermines the independence of the Election Commission.

articles.title: SC pauses board appointments in firm linked to Sunjay Kapur
articles.description: The Supreme Court urged mediation in the family dispute over Sunjay Kapur's estate, halting a board meeting to prevent further tensions.

articles.title: Devji: This former top Maoist, who had a ₹1 crore bounty on his head, is now appearing for exams after 40 years and wants to become a lawyer
articles.description: Nearly four decades after abandoning his studies, former Maoist leader Devji, 63, reappeared for his Telugu Intermediate exam. He secured special permission to complete his education, which was disrupted in 1983 due to police intimidation during exams. Devji now plans to pursue law to advocate for marginalized communities.

articles.title: What makes the Rs 90,000-crore Great Nicobar Island project critical for India both militarily and economically
articles.description: India is undertaking a massive Rs 90,000 crore project in Great Nicobar. This initiative will develop a major transshipment port and international airport. It aims to strengthen India's maritime presence and trade infrastructure. The project is strategically located near the Malacca Strait, a vital global trade route. Defence veterans support its importance for national security and economic growth.

articles.title: Mallikarjun Kharge calls fuel price hike "Modi-govt-made crisis," alleges leadership failure amid West Asia conflict
articles.description: Congress leader Mallikarjun Kharge criticizes the central government over rising petrol and diesel prices. He labels the situation a 'Modi-government-made crisis' and blames leadership failure for the economic burden on citizens. Kharge highlights the cascading effect of inflation from fuel price increases. Union Minister Kiren Rijiju defends the hike, stating India's increases are lower than many other nations.

articles.title: 40 years ago May 15, 1986: PM in Africa
articles.description: This is the front page of The Indian Express published on May 15, 1986.

articles.title: Trump’s final day in China: Tea diplomacy with Xi Jinping after high-stakes talks
articles.description: US President Trump concludes his 2026 Beijing visit with informal talks with Xi Jinping. Read our full summary of the trade and security discussions here.

articles.title: 'Iran NOT Going Anywhere': Saudi's Bold 'ALLIANCE' Offer To Tehran JOLTS Israel & U.S.
articles.description: Saudi Arabia is quietly promoting the idea of a regional non-aggression pact with Iran and other Middle Eastern states to help stabilize the region once the ongoing US-Israeli war against Tehran ends. Diplomats say Riyadh is looking at the 1975 Helsinki Accords — a Cold War-era agreement that eased tensions between rival powers — as a possible framework for the initiative.#saudi #iran #mbs #mojtabakhamenei #gulf #tehran #riyadh

articles.title: Iran rebukes UAE at BRICS meet, calls it 'aggressor' for attacks
articles.description: At a BRICS meeting in New Delhi, Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister Kazem Gharibabadi accused the UAE of being an 'aggressor' by facilitating military attacks on Iran, stating Tehran had no option but to act in self-defense under the UN Charter.

articles.title: Tripura leads India in regulatory reforms, completes 51 priority areas
articles.description: Tripura becomes India's first state to complete all 51 Priority Areas under the national Compliance Reduction and Deregulation initiative. This milestone positions Tripura as a leader in regulatory reforms, Ease of Doing Business, and tech governance.

articles.title: Noida Protest Accused Got Over Rs 1 Crore From Foreign Countries: Cops
articles.description: The law and order situation had deteriorated during violent protest in Noida in April.

articles.title: 'U.S. Behind Instability': Russia Slams Trump's Iran War; Bats For Gulf Stability | WATCH
articles.description: Russian Security Council Secretary Sergei Shoigu stated that the West has misappropriated assets belonging to other states worth over half a trillion dollars, speaking at a meeting of the Shanghai Co-operation Organisation (SCO) in Bishkek on Thursday. Shoigu expressed confidence that 'the leaders will draw the correct conclusions' about the risks of holding national savings in Western banks. Additionally, the Secretary of the Russian Security Council noted that the global situation continues to 'steadily worsen' due to the United States' desire to preserve its status as a dominant power. Watch.

articles.title: UP adopts curbs to save fuel, BJP state chief urges lifestyle changes
articles.description: Chairman of Uttar Pradesh Legislative Council Kunwar Manvendra Singh has postponed all previously scheduled tour programmes of the parliamentary committees of the council until further orders. Principal secretary, legislative council, Rajesh Singh confirmed district-level officials would participate in parliamentary committee meetings through video conferencing until further orders.

articles.title: CM Fadnavis’ bike ride draws Opposition ire
articles.description: Maharashtra CM Fadnavis' bike ride sparks opposition criticism amid austerity calls, questioning the government's sincerity and measures post-election.

articles.title: 'I Personally Warned...': Sen. Kelly's Epic Iran Grilling Of U.S. Generals Stuns Hegseth | WATCH
articles.description: US Senator Mark Kelly questioned U.S. Central Command Commander Admiral Brad Cooper and AFRICOM Commander General Dagvin Anderson during a Senate Armed Services Committee hearing, focusing on civilian casualties linked to operations involving Iran and the expanding influence of China and Russia in Africa. Kelly pressed the military leaders on measures to reduce civilian harm and stressed the importance of countering foreign disinformation campaigns across the African continent. Watch the key exchange from Capitol Hill. Watch.

articles.title: India-Israel ties special, unique; rooted in 6 shared values: Envoy
articles.description: On Israel's 78th anniversary, Ambassador Reuven Azar highlighted the 'special and unique' India-Israel relationship. He said the partnership is rooted in six shared values: civilisational resilience, counter-terrorism, and democratic values.

articles.title: Commentary: UMNO has ambitions for Malaysia's next general election but little room for manoeuvre
articles.description: Being part of Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim’s unity government has helped UMNO retain some relevance, but this will bring a different challenge come election time, says Asian Studies professor James Chin.

articles.title: Is Malaysia’s BN gearing up for early state polls in Johor and Melaka with eye on GE?
articles.description: Early elections in these two states would allow Barisan Nasional to drum up momentum and signal that the pact has regained strength ahead of the next general election, observers say.

articles.title: Commentary: Trump’s disinterest not the only reason the Quad is in limbo
articles.description: The Quad risks geopolitical insignificance if summit-level meetings are not resumed, says former foreign correspondent Nirmal Ghosh.

articles.title: Ludhiana: Jagraon bridge elevated road in bad shape, commuters suffer
articles.description: Activist Parminder Mehta, who has been taking up the issue with the municipal corporation, said that the contractor stopped the work about a month ago, soon after starting

articles.title: Villivakkam to get new medical college, better civic infrastructure: Minister Aadhav Arjuna
articles.description: Villivakkam will enhance civic infrastructure with a new medical college and improved safety measures, as announced by Minister Aadhav Arjuna.

articles.title: Keeping the joy of togetherness alive
articles.description: In An Age Of Shrinking Families, The Thakkars Show That One Roof Can House Many Hearts

articles.title: Gujarat: Secretariat staff ask for WFH system
articles.description: Gujarat Sachivalaya Federation urges CM Bhupendra Patel to introduce phased work-from-home, 5-day week and e-sarkar use; cabinet may decide soon.

articles.title: Smelling flowers
articles.description: Flowers offer a unique sensory pleasure. Their scent molecules travel a direct route to the brain, impacting memory and emotions. This triggers neurotransmitters that reduce stress and promote happiness. Early humans were drawn to flowers for survival, a trait now evolved into aesthetic appreciation. Inhaling floral scents creates a powerful neurological connection, transforming simple molecules into profound feelings of joy.

articles.title: Calcutta HC orders police to ensure safe return of post-poll violence victims in Bengal
articles.description: The Calcutta High Court has ordered police to maintain law and order and ensure the safe return of anyone illegally evicted from their shops or homes in West Bengal. This directive comes after allegations of post-poll violence, with former Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee appearing in court.

articles.title: Suvendu Adhikari tightens cattle slaughter rules, cracks down on illegal markets in Bengal
articles.description: The Suvendu Adhikari government has enforced strict regulations on animal slaughter in West Bengal, mandating compliance with the 1950 Act. The order prohibits public slaughter and requires a fitness certificate for animals, with violations punishable by jail time and fines. Additionally, authorities are cracking down on illegal cattle markets and unauthorized slaughterhouses.

articles.title: T HC judge disposes 226 writ petitions in a day, sets record
articles.description: Telangana High Court Justice Surepalli Nanda sets record by disposing 226 writ petitions in one day, including PACS election pleas, land disputes and seizures.

articles.title: Govt restricts all official foreign trips, nixes nods
articles.description: The Goa government has implemented an immediate ban on all official foreign travel for public servants, following Prime Minister Modi's directive to reduce unnecessary expenditures. This decision affects previously approved trips, raising concerns about the tourism department's marketing plans abroad.

articles.title: Mandate Vs. Math: Why Congress Got Kerala Right
articles.description: Ten days, three contenders, one state watching. The story of how Congress almost got its own Kerala moment wrong — and why it pulled back just in time.
